name: TaskIQ

auth.notes: Uses broker auth (Redis, RabbitMQ, etc.) transparently

performance.notes: Async-native; latency depends on broker; Redis broker near real-time

agent_readiness.notes: Modern async-first Python task queue. Native asyncio support (unlike Celery's bolted-on async). Dependency injection via FastAPI-style dependencies in tasks. Multiple brokers: Redis, RabbitMQ, NATS, ZeroMQ, InMemory. Multiple result backends. Middleware system. Kicker pattern for sending tasks. In-memory broker for testing (no infrastructure needed). Type-safe task parameters. FastAPI-compatible architecture.

gotchas: Smaller community than Celery — less third-party tooling
Newer project — fewer battle-tested production deployments
Monitoring dashboard not built-in (use Flower equivalent or custom)
Some brokers have fewer features than Celery equivalents
best_when: FastAPI apps needing async task queues with native asyncio
Dependency injection in task functions (FastAPI-compatible pattern)
Teams wanting a modern Celery alternative for async Python
avoid_when: When extensive Celery ecosystem integrations are needed
summary: TaskIQ is a modern async-first Python task queue with native asyncio support and FastAPI-style dependency injection. Multiple brokers (Redis, RabbitMQ, NATS, InMemory). Type-safe tasks, middleware, and result backends. Best for FastAPI apps needing truly async background tasks — unlike Celery which bolts async onto a sync core. InMemory broker makes testing easy.
